questions.question: The `id -u` option prints only the current group ID.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. id -u option prints only the user ID.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: In `users` output, a user with two login sessions is listed only once.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. In users output, each user name corresponds to a login session, so a user with multiple login sessions appears multiple times.
questions.source_section: 20.5 users: Print login names of users currently logged in
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `users` command prints a blank-separated single-line list of users currently logged in to the current host.
questions.explanation: Correct. users command shows on a single line a blank-separated list of user names of users currently logged in to the current host.
questions.source_section: 20.5 users: Print login names of users currently logged in
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `id --zero` option is allowed when using the default `id` output format.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. id --zero option is not permitted when using the default format.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: Within an existing login session, `id` may fail to reflect changes made later to the group database.
questions.explanation: Correct. After login, id normally does not reflect changes to the group database within the existing login session.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `id -n` option prints names instead of numeric IDs, but it must be used with `-u`, `-g`, or `-G`.
questions.explanation: Correct. id -n option prints the user or group name instead of the ID number and requires -u, -g, or -G.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `id --zero` option uses ASCII NUL characters as output delimiters.
questions.explanation: Correct. id --zero option delimits output items with ASCII NUL characters.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: If a file argument is given to `users`, it must still ignore that file and read only the default login record file.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. If a file argument is given to users, it uses that file instead, and /var/log/wtmp is a common choice.
questions.source_section: 20.5 users: Print login names of users currently logged in
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: By default, `id` never prints any group-related information.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. By default, id shows the real group ID.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: If neither SELinux nor SMACK is enabled, `id -Z` still succeeds quietly with exit status 0.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. If neither SELinux nor SMACK is enabled, id -Z prints a warning and sets the exit status to 1.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: Running `groups` with explicit user names causes the user and group database to be consulted afresh.
questions.explanation: Correct. Running groups with a list of users causes the user and group database to be consulted afresh.
questions.source_section: 20.4 groups: Print group names a user is in
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: After login, `groups` always reflects any group-database changes immediately within the existing login session.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. After login, groups normally does not reflect changes to the group database within the existing login session.
questions.source_section: 20.4 groups: Print group names a user is in
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: If `who` is given one non-option argument, it uses that file instead of the default system-maintained file.
questions.explanation: Correct. If given one non-option argument, who uses that file instead of a default system-maintained file.
questions.source_section: 20.6 who: Print who is currently logged in
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: By default, `id` omits the real user ID and shows only the effective user ID.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. By default, id shows the real user ID.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `id -r` option switches output to real IDs instead of effective IDs, and it requires `-u`, `-g`, or `-G`.
questions.explanation: Correct. id -r option prints the real instead of effective user or group ID and requires -u, -g, or -G.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: Running `id` with a user argument causes the user and group database to be consulted afresh.
questions.explanation: Correct. Running id with a user argument causes the user and group database to be consulted afresh.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The group list printed by `groups` is equivalent to the output of `id -Gn`.
questions.explanation: Correct. group lists printed by groups are equivalent to the output of id -Gn.
questions.source_section: 20.4 groups: Print group names a user is in
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `id -Z` option prints the process security context together with the normal default `id` fields.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. id -Z option prints only the security context of the process.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `groups` command prints primary and supplementary group names for a given user, or for the current process if no names are given.
questions.explanation: Correct. groups command shows the names of the primary and any supplementary groups for each given username, or for the current process if no names are given.
questions.source_section: 20.4 groups: Print group names a user is in
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `id` command can print information about a named user or about the running process when no user is specified.
questions.explanation: Correct. id command shows information about the given user, or about the running process if no user is specified.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: When `groups` is given more than one user name, it prints only the group lists and never prefixes them with user names.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. If more than one name is given to groups, the name of each user is printed before that user's group list, separated by a colon.
questions.source_section: 20.4 groups: Print group names a user is in
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: With no file argument, `users` reads its information from a system-maintained file.
questions.explanation: Correct. With no file argument, users extracts its information from a system-maintained file.
questions.source_section: 20.5 users: Print login names of users currently logged in
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `who --count` option prints only the login names and the number of users logged on, and it overrides all other options.
questions.explanation: Correct. who --count option prints only the login names and the number of users logged on, and overrides all other options.
questions.source_section: 20.6 who: Print who is currently logged in
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: By default, `id` prints the effective user ID only when it differs from the real user ID.
questions.explanation: Correct. By default, id shows the effective user ID if it differs from the real user ID.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `id -g` option prints only the user ID.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. id -g option prints only the group ID.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: With two non-option arguments such as `am i`, `who` prints every logged-in user but omits the hostname.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. If given two non-option arguments, who prints only the entry for the user running it, preceded by the hostname.
questions.source_section: 20.6 who: Print who is currently logged in
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: With no non-option arguments, `who` prints login name, terminal line, login time, and remote hostname or X display for each logged-on user.
questions.explanation: Correct. With no non-option arguments, who prints login name, terminal line, login time, and remote hostname or X display for each user currently logged on.
questions.source_section: 20.6 who: Print who is currently logged in
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: For `id`, a plain numeric-looking argument is always treated as a user ID even when a matching user name exists.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. For id, user name lookup takes precedence over interpreting a value as a user ID unless the ID is specified with a leading '+'.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `who --boot` option prints the current number of logged-in users.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. who --boot option prints the date and time of last system boot.
questions.source_section: 20.6 who: Print who is currently logged in
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `id -G` option prints only the user ID and omits all group information.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. id -G option prints only the group ID and the supplementary groups.
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.
